mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs within the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive mean forward return over 60 trading days across multiple expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals manufacturing weakness that depresses equity valuations over the subsequent month during economic slowdowns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or flat when industrial production contracts during confirmed slowdown periods, invalidating the supply-demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Steady monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects consistent economic momentum that supports near-term equity price appreciation over the following week.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Rank IC for payroll growth against 5-day forward returns remains negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with stable federal funds rates indicate unanchored inflation expectations that erode real corporate margins and suppress equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets generate positive 60-day returns when CPI accelerates while policy rates remain unchanged, contradicting the real margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate falling below 1.5 percent signals insufficient aggregate demand to sustain earnings growth, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns exceed zero in more than 60% of quarters where GDP growth drops below the 1.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in industrial production during periods of monetary tightening amplify supply-side constraints that force immediate equity repricing downward within the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production drops during tightening cycles fail to predict negative 5-day equity returns, with hit rates below 45% over walk-forward tests.
